This is your much more expensive model, and it should be optimal at that point. Okay, so let's dive into pre-training scaling laws a bit more. Here's an example, a very common example that everyone uses. This is called the Trenchilla scaling law. So here, the goal is to model the loss, so how good your model is, a lower loss is better, as a function of two things, the number of parameters and the number of tokens. And as I alluded to, there's kind of an optimal rule of thumb that people use, which is roughly 20 tokens per parameter. And basically everyone uses this, all of these industry labs and so on.
